name: Urine output
description: Urine output — the volume of urine produced, as a rate or over a time window (hourly, daily) and by collection route (catheter, nephrostomy); reported in mL (or mL/h, mL/kg/h).

concept.conceptName: Urine output by in and out urethral catheter

en.name: Urine output
en.shortDescription: Urine output — the volume of urine produced, as a rate or over a time window (hourly, daily) and by collection route (catheter, nephrostomy); reported in mL (or mL/h, mL/kg/h).
en.longDescription:
## Definition & Clinical Context

Urine output is the volume of urine the patient produces, used to assess kidney function and fluid balance. It is one of the most closely watched parameters in the intensive care unit, where a falling urine output is an early sign of acute kidney injury or hypovolaemia. Measuring it produces a volume (in millilitres) collected over a defined period — most often hourly from an indwelling urinary catheter with a graduated collection chamber — and is frequently expressed as a rate (mL/h, or mL/kg/h normalised to body weight). Outside critical care it is also recorded as a 24-hour total or as the volume of individual voids.

This concept set captures **urine-volume measurements**: the generic urine output, fixed-window totals (1, 8, 10, 12, 24 hour), route-specific outputs (urethral catheter, nephrostomy), and the SNOMED urine-volume family (hourly, timed, voided, single-void volumes). The time window and collection route are preserved by the specific concept chosen.

## Included Concepts

This concept set contains 19 standard concepts (LOINC + SNOMED). The SNOMED side is anchored on *4264378 / 364202003 — Measure of urine output (SNOMED)* with descendants; the LOINC side is a curated list. The default for an unqualified urine-output value is *3014315 / 9187-6 — Urine output (LOINC)*.

### Generic urine output

- *3014315 / 9187-6 — Urine output (LOINC)*: The generic urine output, no time window or route specified. Default mapping target.
- *4264378 / 364202003 — Measure of urine output (SNOMED)*, *37397937 / 993701000000102 — Urine volume (SNOMED)*, *40458249 / 251842000 — Urine output volume (SNOMED)*, *37394139 / 1027231000000106 — Other urine volume (SNOMED)*: SNOMED concepts for a urine-volume measurement without a specific window; equivalent to the generic LOINC concept.
- *4247279 / 60309003 — Diuresis (SNOMED)*: "Diuresis" is widely used (especially in French — *diurèse*) as a synonym for urine output; included as a generic mapping target.

### By time window

These record the urine volume accumulated over a fixed period. In the ICU the 1-hour value is the workhorse measurement.

- *3014835 / 9188-4 — Urine output 1 hour (LOINC)* and *4145209 / 426543000 — Hourly urine volume (SNOMED)*: Hourly urine output.
- *3008828 / 9189-2 — Urine output 8 hour (LOINC)*, *3013533 / 9190-0 — Urine output 10 hour (LOINC)*, *3018727 / 9191-8 — Urine output 12 hour (LOINC)*: Shift-length totals.
- *3008071 / 9192-6 — Urine output 24 hour (LOINC)*, *4191836 / 395060000 — 24 hour urine output (SNOMED)*, *4227851 / 404232001 — 24 hour voided urinary volume (SNOMED)*: Daily totals.
- *4227850 / 404231008 — Timed urine volume (SNOMED)*: Volume collected over an unspecified timed period.

### By void

- *4089953 / 251975009 — Voided urinary volume (SNOMED)* and *4090797 / 251977001 — Single voided urinary volume (SNOMED)*: Volume of urine passed in one (or a series of) void(s), as opposed to a continuous catheter collection.

### By collection route

- *3016267 / 9195-9 — Urine output by in and out urethral catheter (LOINC)*: Output measured via an in-and-out (intermittent) urethral catheter.
- *21491173 / 79549-2 — Urine output measured by nephrostomy (LOINC)*: Output drained through a nephrostomy tube (direct kidney drainage), which may be recorded separately from urethral output.

## Excluded Concepts

- *4065542 / 167578005 — Measurement of urine volume over 6 hours (SNOMED, Procedure)*: Removed. This is the procedure (the act of measuring), not the resulting value.
- Therapeutic diuresis interventions (e.g., *4347914 — Alkaline diuresis*, *4121323 — Forced alkaline diuresis*) are deliberately not pulled in: although they are descendants of "Diuresis", they are treatments, not urine-volume measurements. "Diuresis" is therefore included as a single leaf concept, without its descendants.

## Mapping Notes

- *Default concept*: When the source records a urine-output value with no time window or route, map to Urine output (3014315 / 9187-6, LOINC). Do not infer a window that is not stated.
- *Hourly ICU output*: An ICU catheter output recorded each hour maps to Urine output 1 hour (3014835 / 9188-4, LOINC) or Hourly urine volume (4145209 / 426543000, SNOMED). If the source records a running total without a per-hour breakdown, use the matching window (8/12/24 hour) or the generic concept.
- *Rate vs. volume*: Some systems store urine output as a rate (mL/h or mL/kg/h) rather than a raw volume; the concept is the same — preserve the source unit and do not convert.
- *Route*: Map to the nephrostomy (21491173 / 79549-2, LOINC) or in-and-out-catheter (3016267 / 9195-9, LOINC) concept only when the source identifies that collection route; a standard indwelling-catheter output maps to the generic or time-window concept.
- *Common source labels*: "UO", "Urine output", "Diuresis", "Diurèse", "Débit urinaire", "Hourly urine" → Urine output (3014315 / 9187-6, LOINC) or the matching time-window concept.
- *Units*: Urine output is reported in mL, or as a rate in mL/h or mL/kg/h.

fr.name: Diurèse
fr.shortDescription: Diurèse — le volume d'urine produit, en débit ou sur une fenêtre de temps (horaire, journalière) et par voie de recueil (sonde, néphrostomie) ; en mL (ou mL/h, mL/kg/h).

createdByDetails.affiliation: Univ Rennes, CHU Rennes, INSERM, LTSI-UMR 1099, Rennes, France
createdByDetails.profession: Medical Informatics and Intensive Care Physician

versions.author: Boris Delange
versions.message: Anchor the SNOMED side on Measure of urine output (364202003) with descendants (adds hourly/timed/voided urine-volume observables robustly). Keep curated LOINC list (generic + 1h/8h/10h/12h/24h windows + catheter/nephrostomy routes) and the SNOMED Diuresis leaf (without descendants, to avoid pulling in therapeutic-diuresis interventions). Removed the SNOMED procedure "Measurement of urine volume over 6 hours" (167578005, the act). Added clinical description and moved to pending review.
